preview_text:
The model assigns England a 78% chance of victory, leaving DR Congo with only a 3% path to a win. The underdog faces a steep climb; the gap between the two outcomes reflects a heavily favored favorite.

The draw emerges as the most plausible alternative outcome at 19%, lending some competitive texture to the match. England's win probability is substantially higher than either the draw or a DR Congo victory, yet the 19-point margin to the draw suggests the match carries enough uncertainty to avoid being a near-certain blowout.

Model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes, with gaps of 3 percentage points or less. Polymarket pricing sits similarly tight to both. The three sources are in agreement: England is the clear favorite, a draw is credible, and a DR Congo upset remains a longshot.
